meaning: adj.没有补助金的,未受资助(或津贴)的
examples: The unsubsidized selling price of the new iPhone is $399 for the 8 gigabyte model.
T-Mobile could start stocking unsubsidized N900s to show a different way of thinking from the other major carriers.
The unsubsidized selling price of the new iPhone is $399 for the 8 gigabyte model and $499 for the 16 gigabyte device.
They also lower costs at unsubsidized universities by forcing them to hold down tuition to compete.
That unsubsidized price is still too high to compete in most markets with retail electricity without subsidy.
